questions: Identify the medical institution that markets its children's asthma experts by promising to help parents 'breathe easier' when their child gasps and wheezes. Once identified, describe the scope of services offered by this institution's primary care providers, specifically mentioning the conditions and screenings covered, and list the three specific body parts focused on by their dermatology team.

rounds.answer_a:
The institution is Texas Children's Hospital, whose "Experts in children's asthma" campaign includes the tagline to help parents "breathe easier" [1]. Their primary care providers, part of the Pediatric Primary Care Group, offer comprehensive services including well-child checkups, sick visits, developmental screenings, and immunizations. They manage common childhood illnesses (like ear infections, strep throat, and flu) and chronic conditions such as asthma, diabetes, and ADHD. Routine screenings cover hearing, vision, developmental milestones, and adolescent health [2]. The hospital's Dermatology Team specifically focuses on three body parts: the skin, hair, and nails [3]. Their specialists treat a wide range of pediatric skin conditions affecting these areas, from common issues like eczema and acne to more complex genetic disorders and birthmarks.

## References
[1] Texas Children's Hospital. "Experts in Children's Asthma." Retrieved from https://www.texaschildrens.org/departments/asthma-allergy
[2] Texas Children's Hospital. "Pediatric Primary Care." Retrieved from https://www.texaschildrens.org/departments/pediatric-primary-care
[3] Texas Children's Hospital. "Dermatology." Retrieved from https://www.texaschildrens.org/departments/dermatology
rounds.answer_b:
Cleveland Clinic Children’s markets its pediatric asthma specialists with the promise to help parents “breathe easier” when their child experiences gasping and wheezing, emphasizing compassionate, expert care for childhood respiratory conditions [1]. The primary care providers at Cleveland Clinic Children’s offer comprehensive services focused on preventive medicine, acute illness management, and chronic condition support. They provide routine well-child visits, developmental screenings, immunizations, and management of common pediatric issues such as asthma, allergies, infections, and behavioral or mental health concerns [2]. These providers work closely with pediatric subspecialists to ensure coordinated, family-centered care. The dermatology team at Cleveland Clinic Children’s specializes in diagnosing and treating skin conditions in pediatric patients, with a particular focus on the skin, hair, and nails [3]. Their services include care for eczema, psoriasis, acne, birthmarks, and genetic or autoimmune skin disorders, delivered through a multidisciplinary approach tailored to children’s unique needs. By integrating advanced medical treatments with patient education and family support, the institution ensures holistic care across multiple pediatric specialties.

## References  
[1] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/childrens-hospital  
[2] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/childrens-hospital/primary-care  
[3] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/childrens-hospital/dermatology

Agent A failed this fundamental step, identifying Texas Children's Hospital instead. Because Agent A identified the wrong institution, all subsequent details regarding primary care and dermatology were incorrect for the requested entity.
